body.title: Granny: CC dispatch_fn — GRANNY_DISPATCH channel post on route
body.result: cc_dispatch_fn: cc_queue dispatch + GRANNY_DISPATCH channel post; wired in GrannyShim.start(); 4 tests green
body.description:
GrannyWeatherwaxDevice._DEFAULT_ROUTING maps Platform/Infrastructure → cc, but routing edges have dispatch_fn=None. When dispatch_fn is None, route_ticket() falls back to a plain channel post (no actual dispatch). For the factory loop to work, the CC routing edges need a real dispatch_fn that: (1) calls cc_queue.py dispatch <ticket_id> to set ticket in_progress, (2) posts GRANNY_DISPATCH|ticket=T-xxx|worker=claude|title=... to the shared channel so the CC task listener can pick it up.

Wire the dispatch_fn at GrannyShim startup (or in a GrannyDaemon.__init__) by calling device.register_worker('cc', ['Platform','Infrastructure','Cognition','Database','Training'], dispatch_fn=_cc_dispatch_fn). The _cc_dispatch_fn should call cc_queue.py dispatch + post to channel.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/shim.py (add dispatch_fn registration in start()), devices/granny/device.py (dispatch_fn receives ticket dict)
**Design rules:** no SQLite; channel post via unseen_university.channel
**Scope boundary:** only the dispatch_fn for cc worker. Do not change routing logic or edge weights.
**Test plan:** mock cc_queue dispatch; call route_ticket({'id':'T-test','tags':['Platform'],...}); assert channel received GRANNY_DISPATCH message with ticket id
